pages_context.page_text:
THIS LEASE AGREEMENT (the "Lease") is made and entered into this 11th day of 
March, 2026 (the "Effective Date"), by and between Downstate at LICH Holding 
Company, Inc., a New York corporation, having its principal place of business at 1290 
Avenue of the Americas, New York, NY 10104 (hereinafter referred to as "Landlord"), 
and DHL Express (USA), Inc., an Ohio corporation, having its principal place of 
business at 1210 South Pine Island Road, Plantation, FL 33324 (hereinafter referred to 
as "Tenant").

WITNESSETH:

ARTICLE 1: LEASED PREMISES 1.01 Description of Premises: Landlord, in 
consideration of the rents to be paid and the covenants to be performed by Tenant, 
does hereby lease to Tenant, and Tenant hereby leases from Landlord, that certain real 
property and all improvements thereon located at 74 Amity Street, Brooklyn, NY 
11201. 1.02 Property Specifics: The premises consist of a four-story shell building 
totaling approximately 5,431 square feet of gross building area , situated on a 
rectangular lot of approximately 1,875 square feet (0.04 acres). The property is 
identified on the Tax Map of the City of New York as Block 295, Lot 7. 1.03 Condition 
"As-Is": Tenant acknowledges that it has inspected the premises and accepts them in 
their current "As-Is" condition. Tenant acknowledges the building is approximately 113 
years old (built circa 1900) and currently exists as a legal, pre-existing, non-
conforming use within the R6/LH-1 zoning district.

ARTICLE 2: TERM

2.01 Initial Term: The initial term of this Lease shall be for a period of fifteen (15) 
years (the "Initial Term"), commencing on May 1, 2026 (the "Commencement Date"), 
and expiring on April 30, 2041, unless sooner terminated or extended as provided 
herein.

2.02 Renewal Options: Tenant shall have the option to extend this Lease for two (2) 
additional periods of ten (10) years each (each a "Renewal Term"). Notice of exercise 
of such option must be delivered to Landlord no later than three hundred sixty-five (365) 
days prior to the expiration of the then-current term.

ARTICLE 3: RENT AND SECURITY DEPOSIT

3.01 Base Rent: Tenant shall pay to Landlord as annual base rent ("Base Rent") for the 
Premises, without setoff or deduction, the following amounts:

• 
Years 1-5: $275,000.00 per annum ($22,916.67 per month).

• 
Years 6-10: $316,250.00 per annum ($26,354.17 per month).

• 
Years 11-15: $363,687.50 per annum ($30,307.29 per month).

3.02 Payment Method: All payments shall be made via ACH transfer to 
Landlord’s designated account or at such other place as Landlord may designate 
in writing.

3.03 Security Deposit: Upon execution of this Lease, Tenant shall deposit with 
Landlord the sum of $100,000.00 (the "Security Deposit"). The Security Deposit 
shall be held by Landlord as security for the faithful performance by Tenant of all 
the terms, covenants, and conditions of this Lease.

3.04 Late Charges: Any installment of Rent or other sum due from Tenant not 
received within five (5) days of its due date shall bear a late charge equal to five 
percent (5%) of the overdue amount.

ARTICLE 4: TAXES AND ASSESSMENTS 4.01 Real Property Taxes: This is a "Triple 
Net" (NNN) Lease. Tenant shall be responsible for the payment of all real property taxes 
and assessments levied against the property. For reference, the 2013/2014 taxable 
assessment was $206,820, with a base tax liability of $27,261. Tenant acknowledges 
that future assessments will likely increase significantly following DHL's planned 
industrial build-out. 4.02 Payment of Taxes: Tenant shall pay all such taxes directly to 
the City of New York Assessing Authority at least fifteen (15) days prior to the 
delinquency date and provide Landlord with evidence of payment.

ARTICLE 5: USE OF PREMISES 5.01 Permitted Use: Tenant shall use and occupy the 
Premises solely for a last-mile logistics and distribution center, including but not limited to 
package sorting, courier dispatch, fleet vehicle parking (subject to local zoning), administrative 
offices, and temporary storage of goods in transit. 5.02 Compliance with Laws: Tenant shall, at 
its sole cost and expense, comply with all Laws, including the Americans with Disabilities Act 
(ADA) and New York City Building Codes. Tenant specifically acknowledges the 
Landmark/Historic District (LH-1) status of the neighborhood and agrees that any exterior 
modifications must be approved by the NYC Landmarks Preservation Commission (LPC). 5.03 
Hazardous Materials: Tenant shall not cause or permit any Hazardous Materials to be brought 
upon, kept, or used in or about the Premises, except for cleaning supplies and vehicle fluids 
(motor oil, coolant) handled in accordance with all Environmental Laws.

ARTICLE 6: INSURANCE 6.01 Tenant’s Liability Insurance: Tenant shall maintain 
Commercial General Liability Insurance with a limit of not less than $10,000,000.00 per 
occurrence and $20,000,000.00 annual aggregate. Such policy shall name Landlord and its 
mortgagees as "Additional Insureds." 6.02 Property Insurance: Tenant shall maintain "All 
Risk" property insurance covering the building and all improvements for 100% of the full 
replacement cost, currently estimated at $2,850,000.00. 6.03 Fleet and Cargo Insurance: 
Tenant shall maintain Business Automobile Liability insurance covering all owned, hired, and 
non-owned vehicles used in connection with its operations at 74 Amity Street, with a limit of 
$5,000,000.00 per accident. 6.04 Waiver of Subrogation: Landlord and Tenant each hereby 
waive any and all rights of recovery against the other for loss or damage to such waiving party or 
its property, which loss or damage is covered by valid and collectible insurance policies.

ARTICLE 7: MAINTENANCE AND REPAIRS 7.01 Tenant’s Obligations: This is a "Total 
Net" lease. Tenant shall, at its own cost and expense, keep and maintain the entire Premises in 
good condition and repair, including but not limited to:

• 
Structural Elements: The foundation, load-bearing walls, and floor slabs.

• 
Building Systems: All HVAC, electrical, plumbing, and fire suppression systems 
(including the installation of a new dry-pipe sprinkler system if required for high-piled 
storage).

• 
Exterior: The roof membrane, gutters, downspouts, and the historic facade (subject to 
LPC guidelines). 7.02 Landlord’s Obligations: Landlord shall have zero maintenance 
obligations under this Lease. Any repairs necessitated by the age of the building 
(constructed 1900) are the sole responsibility of the Tenant.

ARTICLE 8: ALTERATIONS AND IMPROVEMENTS 8.01 Initial Build-Out: Landlord 
grants Tenant the right to perform a comprehensive "Logistics Retrofit" of the shell building. 
This includes:

• 
Installation of an industrial-grade freight elevator (min. 5,000 lbs capacity).

• 
Reinforcement of the second and third-floor joists to support a live load of 150 lbs/sq. ft.

• 
Modification of the ground floor facade to include a recessed loading bay with an 
automated roll-up door (subject to Article 5.02). 8.02 Approval of Plans: Before 
commencing any work exceeding $50,000.00 in cost, Tenant shall submit detailed plans 
and specifications to Landlord for written approval, which shall not be unreasonably 
withheld, conditioned, or delayed. 8.03 Liens: Tenant shall keep the Premises free from 
any mechanic’s liens or other liens arising out of any work performed, materials 
furnished, or obligations incurred by or for Tenant.

ARTICLE 9: UTILITIES AND SERVICES 9.01 Utility Responsibility: Tenant shall be 
responsible for and shall pay all charges for water, sewer, gas, electricity, steam, telephone, high-
speed fiber-optic data, and all other utility services used by Tenant in the Premises. 9.02 
Upgrades to Electrical Service: Tenant acknowledges that the existing electrical service 
(currently estimated at 200 Amps) is insufficient for its sorting conveyor systems and electric 
vehicle (EV) charging stations. Tenant is authorized, at its sole cost, to upgrade the service to a 
minimum of 1,200 Amps, including the installation of a new transformer vault if required by 
Con Edison. 9.03 HVAC Systems: Tenant shall install and maintain its own climate control 
systems. Given the "shell" nature of the building, Tenant shall provide adequate ventilation and 
heating to prevent the freezing of pipes and the degradation of the historic brickwork.

ARTICLE 10: SIGNS AND ADVERTISING 10.01 Signage Rights: Tenant shall have the 
right to install its standard "DHL Express" corporate branding on the exterior of the building, 
subject to the following:

• 
LPC Approval: As the property is located within the Cobble Hill Historic District, any 
signage must be approved by the NYC Landmarks Preservation Commission (LPC).

• 
Dimensions: Signage shall not exceed 24 square feet in total area.

• 
Lighting: No neon or flashing signs are permitted. Internal illumination or "halo" 
lighting is subject to Landlord’s prior written consent. 10.02 Directory: Tenant shall be 
entitled to one (1) listing on any building directory, should Landlord choose to install 
one.

ARTICLE 11: PARKING, LOADING, AND LOGISTICS 11.01 Loading Zone 
Management: Tenant acknowledges that Amity Street is a narrow residential corridor. Tenant 
agrees to coordinate all deliveries to minimize traffic congestion. 11.02 Curb Cuts and 
Sidewalks: Tenant may apply for a 15-foot curb cut to facilitate the entry of "Step Van" 
delivery vehicles into the ground-floor loading bay. All costs for sidewalk reinforcement to 
handle heavy vehicle weight shall be borne by the Tenant. 11.03 EV Infrastructure: Landlord 
grants Tenant the right to install up to eight (8) Level-2 EV charging stations within the ground 
floor interior to support DHL’s "GoGreen" electric fleet initiative. 11.04 Sidewalk 
Maintenance: Tenant shall be responsible for keeping the sidewalk in front of 74 Amity Street 
free of snow, ice, and debris at all times, in accordance with NYC Administrative Code.

ARTICLE 12: ACCESS BY LANDLORD 12.01 Right of Entry: Landlord and its agents shall 
have the right to enter the Premises at all reasonable times, upon no less than twenty-four (24) 
hours' prior notice (except in emergencies), for the purpose of:

• 
Inspecting the condition of the Premises.

• 
Showing the Premises to prospective purchasers or lenders.

• 
Performing any repairs Landlord may be required or permitted to perform under this 
Lease. 12.02 DHL Security Protocol: Landlord acknowledges that the Premises contain 
high-value international cargo. Landlord agrees to comply with DHL’s TSA-mandated 
security protocols, including signing in at the security desk and being escorted by a 
DHL employee at all times during any entry.

ARTICLE 13: ASSIGNMENT AND SUBLETTING 13.01 Consent Required: Tenant shall 
not assign this Lease or sublet any portion of the Premises without Landlord's prior written 
consent, which shall not be unreasonably withheld. 13.02 Permitted Transfers: 
Notwithstanding Article 13.01, Tenant may assign this Lease or sublease the Premises to any 
Affiliate of DHL Express (USA), Inc. (e.g., Deutsche Post AG or its subsidiaries) without 
Landlord’s consent, provided Tenant gives Landlord ten (10) days' prior notice. 13.03 Profit 
Sharing: In the event of a sublease or assignment to a third party where the rent exceeds the 
Base Rent herein, Tenant shall pay Landlord 50% of such "excess rent."

ARTICLE 14: DAMAGE AND DESTRUCTION 14.01 Casualty Event: If the Premises are 
damaged by fire or other casualty, Tenant shall give immediate notice to Landlord. 14.02 Partial 
Damage: If the Premises are damaged such that they can be repaired within one hundred eighty 
(180) days, Tenant shall, at its expense (using insurance proceeds), restore the Premises. Rent 
shall be abated proportionately to the extent the Premises are unusable for DHL’s logistics 
operations. 14.03 Total Destruction: If the Building is totally destroyed or damaged to the 
extent of more than 50% of its replacement value, either Landlord or Tenant may elect to 
terminate this Lease by giving notice within thirty (30) days of the casualty. 14.04 LPC 
Constraints: Tenant acknowledges that because the property is in a Historic District, any

restoration following a casualty must strictly adhere to the NYC Landmarks Preservation 
Commission (LPC) requirements to maintain the original aesthetic of the 74 Amity Street facade.

ARTICLE 15: CONDEMNATION 15.01 Total Taking: If the entire Premises are taken by 
any public or quasi-public authority under the power of eminent domain, this Lease shall 
terminate as of the date of the taking. 15.02 Partial Taking: If a portion of the Premises is taken 
(e.g., for sidewalk widening on Amity Street) that renders the remainder unsuitable for a high-
volume logistics center, Tenant shall have the right to terminate this Lease. 15.03 Award: 
Landlord shall be entitled to the entire award for any taking, except that Tenant may claim a 
separate award for its moving expenses, the value of its "Logistics Retrofit" improvements, and 
its business interruption loss.

ARTICLE 16: DEFAULT AND REMEDIES 16.01 Events of Default: The occurrence of any 
of the following shall constitute an "Event of Default":

• 
Monetary Default: Failure to pay Rent within five (5) days of the due date.

• 
Non-Monetary Default: Failure to perform any other covenant (e.g., maintaining the 
historic facade) within thirty (30) days after written notice.

• 
Insolvency: The filing of a petition for bankruptcy by DHL Express (USA), Inc., or the 
appointment of a receiver for Tenant’s assets. 16.02 Landlord’s Remedies: Upon an 
Event of Default, Landlord may:

• 
Terminate this Lease and recover possession of the Premises.

• 
Re-let the Premises and hold Tenant liable for any deficiency in rent.

• 
Accelerate the remaining Rent due under the term, discounted to present value at a rate of 
4% per annum. 16.03 Cure Rights: Tenant shall have the right to cure a monetary 
default twice in any twelve-month period. A third occurrence shall be deemed an 
"Incurable Default."

ARTICLE 17: ESTOPPEL CERTIFICATES AND SUBORDINATION 17.01 Estoppel 
Certificate: Within ten (10) days of a request by Landlord, Tenant shall execute and deliver a 
statement certifying that this Lease is in full force and effect, the date to which Rent has been 
paid, and whether any defaults exist. 17.02 Subordination: This Lease is and shall be 
subordinate to any mortgage now or hereafter placed upon the Premises, provided that the 
mortgagee enters into a Non-Disturbance Agreement (SNDA) with Tenant, ensuring that 
DHL’s possession will not be disturbed so long as it is not in default.

ARTICLE 18: INDEMNIFICATION 18.01 Tenant Indemnity: Tenant shall indemnify, 
defend, and hold Landlord harmless from and against any and all claims, losses, or liabilities 
(including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising from Tenant’s use of the Premises, the conduct of 
its business, or any activity permitted or suffered by Tenant in or about the Premises. 18.02 
Logistics Liability: Tenant specifically assumes all liability for accidents involving its delivery 
fleet while on the Premises or utilizing the designated loading bay at 74 Amity Street.

ARTICLE 19: ENVIRONMENTAL COMPLIANCE AND HAZARDOUS MATERIALS 
19.01 Baseline Condition: Landlord and Tenant acknowledge that the Building, constructed 
circa 1900, may contain lead-based paint and asbestos-containing materials (ACM). Landlord

has provided the "Summary Appraisal Report" (C&W File ID: 13-12002-902821) as a disclosure 
of known property conditions. 19.02 Tenant Responsibility: Tenant shall not use, manufacture, 
store, or dispose of any Hazardous Materials on the Premises, with the exception of:

• 
Standard office and janitorial supplies.

• 
Automotive fluids contained within DHL delivery vehicles.

• 
Lithium-ion batteries contained within DHL’s electric vehicle fleet and handheld 
scanning devices. 19.03 Remediation: If Tenant’s "Logistics Retrofit" (as described in 
Article 8) requires the disturbance of existing building materials, Tenant shall be 
responsible for the professional abatement of any lead or asbestos in accordance with 
NYC Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) regulations. 19.04 Environmental 
Indemnity: Tenant shall indemnify Landlord against any "Environmental Claims" 
arising from Tenant’s breach of this Article, including the cost of any required 
groundwater or soil remediation.

ARTICLE 20: ADA AND ACCESSIBILITY 20.01 Compliance with ADA: Tenant shall be 
responsible for all costs associated with making the Premises compliant with the Americans with 
Disabilities Act (ADA). 20.02 Specific Modifications: Given the current "shell" state of 74 
Amity Street, Tenant’s build-out must include:

• 
Installation of an ADA-compliant elevator serving all four floors.

• 
At least one (1) ADA-compliant restroom on the ground floor.

• 
Compliant ramps or "zero-entry" thresholds at the Amity Street entrance. 20.03 Historic 
Constraints: If ADA requirements conflict with the NYC Landmarks Preservation 
Commission (LPC) mandates for the facade, Tenant shall seek a "Hardship Exception" or 
"Certificate of Appropriateness" from the LPC.

ARTICLE 21: SECURITY AND CARGO PROTECTION 21.01 Tenant’s Security System: 
Tenant is authorized to install an enterprise-grade security system, including CCTV cameras, 
motion sensors, and biometric access control. Exterior cameras must be discreetly mounted to 
comply with historic district aesthetics. 21.02 TSA and Customs Compliance: As a "Certified 
Cargo Screening Facility," Tenant must maintain strict access control. Tenant shall have the right 
to exclude Landlord or its agents from high-security "Sensitive Compartmented Information" or 
"Secure Cargo" areas unless escorted by a DHL Security Officer. 21.03 Vault and Storage: 
Tenant may install a high-security vault or "cage" on the second floor for the storage of high-
value international shipments (e.g., jewelry, electronics, and sensitive documents).

ARTICLE 22: NOISE AND VIBRATION CONTROL 22.01 Acoustic Mitigation: To 
preserve the residential character of Amity Street, Tenant shall install sound-dampening 
insulation around any sorting conveyors or heavy machinery. 22.02 Vibration Limits: Tenant’s 
operations, specifically the movement of heavy freight elevators and sorting equipment, shall not 
cause vibrations that exceed the NYC Noise Code or threaten the structural integrity of the 113-
year-old load-bearing brick walls. 22.03 Hours of Operation: While DHL may operate 24/7 for 
sorting purposes, exterior loading and unloading activities via the Amity Street bay shall be 
limited to the hours of 6:00 AM to 9:00 PM to minimize neighborhood disturbance.

pages_context.page_heading: ARTICLE 23: FIRE AND LIFE SAFETY SYSTEMS
pages_context.page_text:
ARTICLE 23: FIRE AND LIFE SAFETY SYSTEMS 23.01 System Installation: Tenant 
shall install a comprehensive fire alarm and life safety system throughout the four-story 
structure, monitored 24/7 by a UL-listed central station. 23.02 Sprinkler Requirements: Tenant 
shall install a "Wet Pipe" or "Pre-Action" sprinkler system throughout all floors, designed to 
meet NFPA 13 standards for "Ordinary Hazard Group 2" occupancy. 23.03 Landlord 
Inspection: Landlord shall be provided with copies of all annual fire safety inspection 
certificates and "Fitness of System" reports.

ARTICLE 24: SUBORDINATION, NON-DISTURBANCE, AND ATTORNMENT 
(SNDA) 24.01 Subordination: Subject to the provisions of Section 24.02, this Lease and all 
rights of Tenant hereunder are and shall be subject and subordinate to any ground lease, 
mortgage, deed of trust, or other lien (each, a "Mortgage") now or hereafter existing against the 
Premises. 24.02 Non-Disturbance: Landlord shall obtain for the benefit of Tenant a Non-
Disturbance Agreement from any current or future Mortgagee. Such agreement shall provide 
that, so long as Tenant is not in default of its obligations under this Lease, Tenant’s possession 
and rights under this Lease shall not be disturbed or terminated by any foreclosure or other 
proceeding initiated by the Mortgagee. 24.03 Attornment: If any Mortgagee (or a purchaser at a 
foreclosure sale) succeeds to the rights of Landlord, Tenant shall "attorn" to such successor 
(recognize them as the new Landlord). Tenant shall continue to perform all of its obligations 
under this Lease without any change in terms, provided the successor Landlord assumes all of 
the obligations of the Landlord herein.

ARTICLE 25: TRANSFER OF LANDLORD’S INTEREST 25.01 Sale of Premises: 
Landlord shall have the right to sell, transfer, or assign its interest in the Premises at any time. 
Upon such transfer, the transferring Landlord shall be automatically released from all liability for 
the performance of the covenants of this Lease occurring after the date of such transfer, provided 
that the new owner assumes such obligations in writing. 25.02 Transfer of Security Deposit: In 
the event of a sale or transfer, Landlord shall transfer the $100,000.00 Security Deposit to the 
new owner. Upon such transfer and the new owner’s acknowledgment of receipt, the original 
Landlord shall be released from all liability regarding the Security Deposit.

ARTICLE 26: ESTOPPEL CERTIFICATES 26.01 Tenant’s Obligation: Within ten (10) 
business days following a written request by Landlord or any Mortgagee, Tenant shall execute 
and deliver an Estoppel Certificate. 26.02 Contents: The Estoppel Certificate shall certify:

• 
That this Lease is unmodified and in full force and effect (or stating modifications).

• 
The dates to which Base Rent and Additional Rent have been paid.

• 
That, to the best of Tenant’s knowledge, no default exists on the part of Landlord or 
Tenant.

• 
Such other factual matters as Landlord or a prospective purchaser/lender may reasonably 
request. 26.03 Failure to Deliver: Tenant’s failure to deliver the certificate within the 
required timeframe shall be deemed a material default under this Lease.

ARTICLE 27: QUIET ENJOYMENT 27.01 Landlord’s Covenant: Landlord covenants and 
agrees that, upon Tenant paying the Rent and performing all the covenants and conditions of this

Lease, Tenant shall peaceably and quietly have, hold, and enjoy the Premises for the Term, free 
from any disturbance by Landlord or any person claiming through or under Landlord.

ARTICLE 28: MORTGAGEE PROTECTION 28.01 Notice of Default: Tenant agrees to 
give any Mortgagee (whose address has been provided to Tenant) a copy of any notice of default 
served upon the Landlord. 28.02 Right to Cure: Tenant further agrees that such Mortgagee shall 
have a period of thirty (30) days after receipt of such notice to cure the Landlord’s default 
before Tenant may exercise any remedy (such as lease termination or rent set-off, if applicable).

ARTICLE 29: SURRENDER OF PREMISES 29.01 Surrender Condition: Upon the 
expiration or earlier termination of this Lease, Tenant shall surrender the Premises to Landlord in 
"Broom Clean" condition and in good order and repair, reasonable wear and tear and damage by 
casualty excepted. 29.02 Removal of Tenant’s Property: Tenant shall remove all of its personal 
property, including but not limited to:

• 
All DHL-branded sorting conveyors and automation equipment.

• 
All EV charging stations and related electrical infrastructure (unless Landlord requests 
they remain).

• 
All telecommunications, server racks, and security hardware. 29.03 Restoration of 
Alterations: At the time of Landlord’s approval of any Alterations (under Article 8), 
Landlord shall specify whether Tenant is required to remove such Alterations upon 
surrender.

• 
The Freight Elevator: Tenant shall leave the industrial freight elevator in good working 
order.

• 
Loading Bay: Tenant shall be required to restore the exterior facade to its original 
"Shell" configuration (removing the roll-up door and restoring the historic masonry) if 
requested by Landlord at least six (6) months prior to expiration. 29.04 Environmental 
Exit Audit: Upon surrender, Tenant shall provide Landlord with an "Environmental 
Close-out Report" from a certified environmental consultant, confirming that no 
Hazardous Materials (leaked fuel, battery acid, etc.) remain on site.

ARTICLE 30: HOLDING OVER 30.01 Holdover Status: If Tenant remains in possession of 
the Premises after the expiration of the Term without the execution of a new lease or a written 
extension, Tenant shall be deemed to be occupying the Premises as a tenant-at-sufferance only. 
30.02 Holdover Rent: During any such holdover period, Tenant shall pay Base Rent at a rate 
equal to one hundred fifty percent (150%) of the Base Rent in effect during the last month of 
the Term. 30.03 Indemnity for Holdover: Tenant shall indemnify and hold Landlord harmless 
from and against any and all loss, cost, or damage (including "consequential damages" such as 
the loss of a new high-value tenant) resulting from Tenant’s failure to timely surrender the 
Premises.

ARTICLE 31: ABANDONMENT 31.01 Definition: Tenant shall be deemed to have 
abandoned the Premises if Tenant (a) vacates the Premises for a period of thirty (30) consecutive 
days and (b) fails to pay Rent. 31.02 Landlord’s Rights: In the event of abandonment, Landlord 
may enter the Premises, remove any remaining property (which shall be deemed abandoned by 
Tenant), and re-let the Premises without terminating this Lease.

ARTICLE 32: RELOCATION RIGHTS 32.01 Landlord’s Right to Relocate: (Intentionally 
Omitted). Landlord acknowledges that the specific location of 74 Amity Street, its historic shell, 
and its proximity to the Brooklyn-Queens Expressway (BQE) and the East River waterfront are 
unique to DHL’s last-mile delivery network; therefore, Landlord shall have no right to relocate 
Tenant to other premises.

ARTICLE 33: BROKERAGE 33.01 Broker Identification: Landlord and Tenant represent 
and warrant to each other that they have dealt with no broker in connection with this Lease other 
than Cushman & Wakefield, Inc. (the "Broker"). 33.02 Commission Payment: Landlord shall 
pay a brokerage commission to the Broker pursuant to a separate written agreement. 33.03 
Mutual Indemnity: Each party agrees to indemnify and hold the other harmless from and 
against any and all claims for brokerage commissions or finder’s fees from any other person or 
entity claiming through such party.

This eighth section focuses on the "Boilerplate" but essential legal protections that govern the 
interpretation of the lease, the methods of communication, and the critical Force Majeure clause 
that protects both parties from unforeseen global or local catastrophes.

COMMERCIAL LEASE AGREEMENT (PART 8 OF 10)

ARTICLE 34: FORCE MAJEURE 34.01 Definition: "Force Majeure" shall mean any event 
beyond the reasonable control of Landlord or Tenant, including but not limited to: acts of God, 
strikes, lockouts, labor disputes, embargoes, fire, cyber-attacks on global logistics networks, 
pandemics, governmental moratoria, or orders of the NYC Landmarks Preservation Commission 
(LPC) that cause unforeseen delays in construction. 34.02 Effect of Force Majeure: If either 
party is delayed or prevented from performing any act required under this Lease (other than the 
payment of Rent) by reason of Force Majeure, the period of such delay shall be deemed an 
excusable delay and the time for performance shall be extended for a period equivalent to the 
period of such delay. 34.03 Exclusion for Rent: Notwithstanding Section 34.02, no event of 
Force Majeure shall excuse Tenant from its obligation to pay Base Rent or Additional Rent when 
due.

ARTICLE 35: NOTICES 35.01 Form of Notice: All notices, demands, or requests required or 
permitted to be given under this Lease must be in writing and shall be deemed given when 
delivered by (a) hand, (b) certified mail, return receipt requested, or (c) a nationally recognized 
overnight courier (such as DHL Express). 35.02 Addresses for Notice:

• 
To Landlord: Downstate at LICH Holding Company, Inc., Attn: General Counsel, 1290 
Avenue of the Americas, New York, NY 10104.

• 
To Tenant: DHL Express (USA), Inc., Attn: Real Estate Department, 1210 South Pine 
Island Road, Plantation, FL 33324. With a copy to: DHL Site Manager, 74 Amity Street, 
Brooklyn, NY 11201.

ARTICLE 36: GOVERNING LAW AND JURISDICTION 36.01 Choice of Law: This 
Lease shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the State of New 
York, without regard to conflict of laws principles. 36.02 Venue: Any legal action or proceeding

arising out of this Lease shall be brought exclusively in the Supreme Court of the State of New 
York, Kings County (Brooklyn), or the United States District Court for the Eastern District of 
New York.

ARTICLE 37: WAIVER OF JURY TRIAL 37.01 Mutual Waiver: Landlord and Tenant 
hereby irrevocably waive their respective rights to a trial by jury in any action or proceeding 
involving any claim arising out of or related to this Lease.

ARTICLE 38: RULES AND REGULATIONS 38.01 Compliance: Tenant shall comply with 
the Rules and Regulations attached hereto as Exhibit B. 38.02 Modifications: Landlord reserves 
the right to adopt additional rules for the safety and cleanliness of the building, provided they do 
not unreasonably interfere with DHL’s 24/7 logistics operations.

ARTICLE 39: QUIET ENJOYMENT 39.01 Covenant: Landlord covenants that Tenant, upon 
paying the Rent and performing all of Tenant’s obligations, shall peaceably and quietly have, 
hold, and enjoy the Premises throughout the Term.

ARTICLE 40: NO WAIVER 40.01 Failure to Enforce: The failure of either party to insist 
upon strict performance of any covenant shall not be construed as a waiver of such covenant in 
the future. The receipt by Landlord of Rent with knowledge of a breach shall not be deemed a 
waiver of such breach.

ARTICLE 41: RECORDING 41.01 Memorandum of Lease: Neither party shall record this 
Lease. However, at the request of either party, both parties shall execute and record a 
"Memorandum of Lease" in the Office of the City Register of the City of New York (Kings 
County) to provide public notice of the leasehold interest.

ARTICLE 42: EXHIBIT A - LOGISTICS RETROFIT SPECIFICATIONS 42.01 
Structural Loading Capacities: * Ground Floor: Slab-on-grade must be reinforced to support 
a static load of 500 lbs/sq. ft. to accommodate heavy sorting machinery and the battery weight of 
the EV fleet.

• 
Upper Floors (2-4): Tenant shall reinforce the existing timber joists with steel "sister" 
beams to achieve a minimum live load capacity of 125 lbs/sq. ft., suitable for palletized 
storage and conveyor tracks. 42.02 Vertical Transportation:

• 
Tenant shall install one (1) high-speed industrial freight elevator with a minimum cabin 
size of 8' x 10' and a weight capacity of 6,000 lbs.

• 
The elevator shaft must be fire-rated for two (2) hours and include a modern "sump 
pump" system in the pit to prevent flooding of the basement level.

ARTICLE 43: EXHIBIT B - ELECTRICAL AND DATA INFRASTRUCTURE 43.01 
Utility Vault: Tenant is authorized to excavate a portion of the basement to create a dedicated 
electrical vault. This vault will house the 1,200 Amp three-phase service equipment. 43.02 EV 
Infrastructure (The "Green Corridor"): * Ground floor wiring must support eight (8) Level-2 
Universal Charging Stations.

• 
Each station must be metered separately to track energy consumption for DHL’s 
"GoGreen" sustainability reporting. 43.03 Fiber-Optic Backbone: Tenant shall run 
redundant fiber-optic lines from the Amity Street utility manhole to a new Server Room 
located on the 4th Floor. This room must be equipped with a dedicated 5-ton Liebert 
cooling unit.

ARTICLE 44: EXHIBIT C - HISTORIC FACADE PRESERVATION PLAN 44.01 Facade 
Restoration: Because 74 Amity Street is a "Four-Story Shell," Tenant agrees to restore the brick 
masonry using "Point-and-Mortar" techniques approved by the NYC Landmarks Preservation 
Commission (LPC). 44.02 The Amity Street Aperture: The ground floor entrance shall be 
modified to include a "Storefront Style" loading door. This door must be finished in DHL 
Yellow (RAL 1032) but designed with architectural moldings that mimic the original 1900s 
carriage house style of the neighborhood. 44.03 Windows: All existing window openings must 
be fitted with double-paned, thermally broken aluminum frames that match the historic "six-
over-six" lite pattern found in the Cobble Hill Historic District.

ARTICLE 45: EXHIBIT D - NOISE AND EMISSION MITIGATION 45.01 Sound Wall: 
Any sorting equipment located within 10 feet of the party walls shared with neighboring 
residential lots must be shielded by an acoustic sound wall with a minimum STC rating of 60. 
45.02 Air Quality: Since DHL will be operating electric vehicles (EVs) inside the building, 
Tenant shall install a high-volume air exchange system capable of 12 air changes per hour 
(ACH) to ensure warehouse employee safety, even though no internal combustion emissions are 
expected.

ARTICLE 46: EXHIBIT E - SITE SECURITY SCHEMATIC 46.01 Perimeter Security: 
All exterior entry points shall be equipped with magnetic locks (1,200 lb holding force) and 
integrated with the DHL Global Security Operations Center (GSOC). 46.02 CCTV Coverage: A 
minimum of 16 IP-based 4K cameras shall be installed, providing 100% coverage of the 
loading bay, the sidewalk interface, and all internal sorting areas.

ARTICLE 47: MISCELLANEOUS PROVISIONS 47.01 Entire Agreement: This Lease, 
including all Exhibits (A through E), contains the entire agreement between the parties. No prior 
oral or written agreements or understandings shall have any force or effect. 47.02 Severability: 
If any provision of this Lease is found to be invalid or unenforceable, the remainder of the Lease 
shall remain in full force and effect. 47.03 Successors and Assigns: The covenants and 
conditions herein shall bind and inure to the benefit of the Landlord and Tenant and their 
respective successors and permitted assigns. 47.04 Counterparts: This Lease may be executed 
in multiple counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together 
shall constitute one and the same instrument. Digital signatures (e.g., DocuSign) shall be deemed 
as valid as original wet signatures.

ARTICLE 48: MEMORANDUM OF LEASE (RECORDING) 48.01 Requirement: 
Simultaneously with the execution of this Lease, the parties shall execute a Memorandum of 
Lease in recordable form. Tenant shall be responsible for the cost of recording such 
Memorandum in the Office of the City Register, Kings County. 48.02 Purpose: The

Memorandum shall set forth the parties, the description of the Premises at 74 Amity Street, the 
Term, and the Renewal Options, without disclosing the specific financial Rent amounts.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties hereto have executed this Lease as of the day and year 
first above written.
